title: calamagrostis arenaria subsp. arundinacea
intro: Calamagrostis arenaria subsp. arundinacea, also known as tufted hairgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Calamagrostis arenaria subsp. arundinacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
